name: Challah (Braided Egg Bread)
recipeIngredient: 1/4 cup (1/2 stick) margarine or butter, room temperature
5 to 5 1/2 cups flour (mix of half all-purpose and half hard wheat white)
recipeInstructions:
1. Make the dough: Lightly grease a large bowl and baking sheet; set aside.
2. In a mixing bowl, combine the first five ingredients and beat until thoroughly blended. Dissolve saffron in water or milk and add to the mixture. Beat in 2 1/4 cups of flour and yeast. Continue beating for 2 minutes, then add an additional 2 1/4 cups of flour or enough to make a soft dough, blending thoroughly.
3. Turn out onto lightly floured board and knead until smooth and elastic, about 8 to 10 minutes. Place in the greased bowl, turning to coat the entire surface. Cover with plastic wrap and a hot, damp towel and let rise in a warm place until doubled in volume.
4. Punch down and let rise again until doubled.
5. Prepare the egg wash: Beat an egg with water.
6. Divide the dough into two equal pieces, then divide each half into three equal pieces. Roll each into a 12-inch rope.
7. Lay three ropes parallel and begin braiding in the middle, working out to each end (make braids very loose). Pinch the ends together securely.
8. Repeat with the remaining ropes.
9. Place loaves on the baking sheet, brush tops with beaten egg, and sprinkle with seeds. Cover with plastic wrap and allow to rise until doubled.
10. Preheat oven to 350°F. Bake loaves until golden and sound hollow when tapped, about 35 to 45 minutes. Cool on racks.
